diagnoses: Acute on chronic systolic heart failure

medications.name: Furosemide
medications.instructions: Take 1 tablet by mouth twice daily

medications.name: Potassium chloride
medications.notes: Take while using furosemide unless told otherwise.

medications.name: Carvedilol
medications.instructions: Take 1 tablet by mouth twice daily with meals

medications.name: Metformin ER
medications.instructions: Take 1 tablet by mouth with dinner
medications.notes: Hold if vomiting or unable to eat.

medications.name: Ibuprofen
medications.notes: Can worsen kidney function and fluid retention.

follow_up: Cardiology follow-up scheduled 03/31/2026 at 09:30 with Heart Failure Clinic.
Primary care follow-up within 1 to 2 weeks.
Basic metabolic panel blood test in 5 to 7 days to check kidney function and potassium.
return_precautions: Call the heart failure clinic for weight gain of 3 lb in 24 hours or 5 lb in one week, worsening swelling, or increased shortness of breath.
Go to the emergency department for severe shortness of breath at rest, chest pain, fainting, confusion, or inability to lie flat due to breathing.
risk_notes: High risk: medication change, diuretic monitoring, kidney disease, and early follow-up required.
